THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE THE FORM OF ORDER. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, NOT REPORTED. (503)

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2017 Continued To Nov-01-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Advanced From Jun-12-17 To Apr-10-17 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Jun-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Asbestos Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Janine M. Fiel-Cosse , Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Eugene McCall.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant as follows:
IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company, in the amount of $74,500.00 in Economic Damages, $300,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $374,500.00.
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Set For Order To Show Cause On Nov-02-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Feb-10-2017 Off Calendar

Plaintiff'S Motion For Preference/ Preferential Setting (Added To Calendar For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Dec-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
(Mini-minutes continued from previous entry)

Expert discovery cut-off date is April 7, 2017. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than January 27, 2017. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than January 4, 2017 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than January 4, 2017 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on February 15, 2017 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on April 10, 2017 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by April 6, 2017 at 2:00p.m. No opposition filed. The prevailing party is to prepare the form of order. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Not Reported. (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Dec-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearance by any party or counsel, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Plaintiff's motion for preference and to advance the trial date is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The June 12, 2017 trial date is advanced to April 10, 2017 at 11:15am in Department 503 with preference status. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is April 20, 2017. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015, now included in Local Rule 20, shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 6, 2017. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than February 24, 2017. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 10, 2017. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by April 3, 2017 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on April 6, 2017. Fact discovery cut-off date is March 24, 2017. (Mini-Minutes Continued, see next entry)

DEPT 206 ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-18-2016 Set For Trial On Jun-12-17 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503
